title: Shali Mata Temple
tagline: Shali Mata Temple — spiritual town of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Shali Mata Temple is a Hindu temple located on stop of Shali Tibba peak, which rises from Khatnol, which is part of Sunni in Shimla district, Himachal Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Shali Mata Temple is a Hindu temple located on stop of Shali Tibba peak, which rises from Khatnol, which is part of Sunni in Shimla district, Himachal Pradesh, India. The base of the trek in Khatnol is about 36 kilometres (22 mi) from the state capital Shimla. It is about 7 kilometres (4.3 mi) trek from Khatnol to Shali Mata Temple. There is no direct motorable road to the temple.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Mashobra
topPlaces.description: Mashobra is a town in Shimla district of Himachal Pradesh. It is connected to the state capital Shimla (erstwhile Simla) through the historic Hindustan–Tibet Road built in 1850 by Lord Dalhousie.

topPlaces.name: Padar, Rewa
topPlaces.description: Padar is a village of Mauganj tehsil in the Mauganj district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is situated near about 8 km south from Mauganj.

topPlaces.name: Chharabra
topPlaces.description: Chharabra is a suburb situated approximately 8250 feet (2514 m) above sea level, 13 km from Shimla, India, on National Highway 22 (Hindustan-Tibet Road). It has the summer retreat of President of India, the summer residence of Governor of Punjab, a five star luxury hotel, a boarding school, and a helipad, even though the population is less than 500 people (including boarding school students and the staff of the president's retreat, governor's house and hotel).

topPlaces.name: Rashtrapati Niwas, Mashobra
topPlaces.description: Rashtrapati Niwas, previously known as The Retreat Building is the official summer retreat of the President of India near Chharabra, Shimla, in the state of Himachal Pradesh. The President stays at the building for at least two weeks during summer and conducts official business.

items.note: Mashobra is a town in Shimla district of Himachal Pradesh. It is connected to the state capital Shimla (erstwhile Simla) through the historic Hindustan–Tibet Road built in 1850 by Lord Dalhousie.
Padar is a village of Mauganj tehsil in the Mauganj district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is situated near about 8 km south from Mauganj.

Chharabra is a suburb situated approximately 8250 feet (2514 m) above sea level, 13 km from Shimla, India, on National Highway 22 (Hindustan-Tibet Road). It has the summer retreat of President of India, the summer residence of Governor of Punjab, a five star luxury hotel, a boarding school, and a helipad, even though the population is less than 500 people (including boarding school students and the staff of the president's retreat, governor's house and hotel).

Rashtrapati Niwas, previously known as The Retreat Building is the official summer retreat of the President of India near Chharabra, Shimla, in the state of Himachal Pradesh. The President stays at the building for at least two weeks during summer and conducts official business.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shali Mata Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shali Mata Temple is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shali Mata Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shimla Airport (Jubbarhatti) (23 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (72 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shali Mata Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shali Mata Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shali Mata Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Shali Mata Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shali Mata Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mashobra, Padar, Rewa, Chharabra, Rashtrapati Niwas, Mashobra.
